vision_summary: The frames depict a chaotic scene on an urban street, characterized by smoke and multiple vehicles. Several cars are visible, some appearing to be involved in an incident. Figures are present, suggesting active movement within the environment.
narration: The stimulus, consisting of 12.5 seconds of high-motion urban activity, elicited significant activation primarily within the bilateral visual networks, specifically involving the left and right hemispheres of the visual cortex (7Networks_LH_Vis and 7Networks_RH_Vis). The distribution of increased |z| scores across multiple visual sub-regions, such as Vis_18, Vis_17, and Vis_25, suggests intensive processing of complex spatial features, motion vectors, and luminance changes inherent in the chaotic scene. This pattern indicates a heightened state of sensory encoding as the brain attempts to parse fragmented visual information and moving objects within the environment. This analysis represents a group-averaged TRIBE v2 prediction, not a diagnostic result.
